steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says the coating is suitable for "common applications" unlike other approaches that require special conditions, and that it reduces friction to nearly nonexistent levels — cutting both energy consumption and wear.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'denies the coating reduces wear': contradicts the passage, which explicitly links near-zero friction to reduced wear. ✗
  • Choice B — 'requires special conditions': the passage states the opposite — the coating works under common conditions, unlike other approaches. ✗
  • Choice C — 'reduces energy consumption and wear in vehicle transmissions and other common mechanical systems': matches both claims — near-zero friction addresses energy loss and wear, and the coating's broad applicability covers common systems. ✓
  • Choice D — 'excludes certain systems': the passage emphasizes broad suitability, not exclusions. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
